name: Belgrad
description: Analog dual-core state variable filter. 10 morphable responses across two resonant peaks. Span detunes the cores up to 8 octaves, balance tilts peak emphasis, TITO switch enables self- or cross-modulation between cores.

controls.name: LEVEL
controls.description: Sets input amplitude into the filter core. Pushes into soft overdrive past unity.
controls.detail: 0–10 · clean below ~6 · progressive input saturation above

controls.name: FREQ
controls.description: Large center-frequency knob for both cores. Scaled 0–10 on the dial.
controls.detail: ~4Hz – 28kHz · sums with V/OCT and attenuated FM

controls.name: SPAN
controls.description: Symmetrically detunes the two filter cores around FREQ. Non-linear taper. Zero = cores aligned, max ≈ 8 oct split.

controls.name: RESO
controls.description: Overall resonance / Q for both cores. Self-oscillates around 8 depending on mode.
controls.detail: 0–10 · self-osc threshold ≈ 8 · sine at resonance, saturates when pushed

controls.name: BALANCE
controls.description: Tilts resonance emphasis between the two peaks. CCW = lower peak dominant · noon = symmetric · CW = upper peak dominant.
controls.detail: bipolar taper · CCW/CW extremes mute the opposite peak

controls.name: FM attenuator (slider)
controls.description: Illuminated slider attenuating the FM input before it reaches FREQ.

controls.name: SPAN CV attenuator (slider)
controls.description: Illuminated slider attenuating the SPAN CV input.

controls.name: TITO
controls.description: 3-way switch routing audio-rate cross-coupling between the cores. Up = SM (self-modulation) · center = off · down = XM (cross-modulation).
controls.detail: SM: textured radical resonance · OFF: clean · XM: warbly chirp / FM-like

controls.name: MODE
controls.description: 10-position rotary selecting the two-core topology. Progresses lowpass → highpass across the dial.
controls.detail: LL · LB · NL · NN · LH · BB · HL · HN · BH · HH

inputs.name: INPUT
inputs.description: Main audio input into the filter, scaled by LEVEL.

inputs.name: V/OCT
inputs.description: 1V/octave pitch input for FREQ. Used when self-oscillating as a sine VCO; tracks ~5 octaves when tuned.

inputs.name: FM
inputs.description: Frequency modulation input into FREQ, scaled by the FM slider.

inputs.name: RESO
inputs.description: CV input for resonance amount. Sums with the RESO knob.

inputs.name: SPAN
inputs.description: CV input for the core detuning amount, scaled by the SPAN slider.

inputs.name: BALANCE
inputs.description: CV input for peak emphasis tilt between lower and upper resonant peaks.

outputs.name: OUTPUT
outputs.description: Main filter output. Level varies significantly with MODE — expect loud bandpass, quiet notch.

behaviors.name: Mode dial morphs LP → HP
behaviors.description: 10 positions ordered so rotation CCW→CW gradually moves from lowpass (LL) to highpass (HH) via bandpass and notch hybrids. LL and HH turn into classic 2-pole vs 4-pole responses at SPAN extremes.

behaviors.name: Dual-peak span detuning
behaviors.description: The two cores are symmetrically offset from FREQ. SPAN=0 collapses to a single peak; SPAN=max spreads up to ~8 octaves. In BB mode this produces two-formant vocal/bowed-string responses.

behaviors.name: Asymmetric peak emphasis
behaviors.description: Tilts resonance strength between the lower and upper peak. Particularly useful in NL and HN modes to expose the notch, and in NN/LH modes to morph between single and dual rejection.

behaviors.name: Self-oscillation as sine VCO
behaviors.description: Both cores produce a clean sine at resonance. V/OCT tracks about 5 octaves when tuned. SPAN sets the interval between the two sine VCOs; BALANCE crossfades between them.

behaviors.name: Nonlinear feedback saturation
behaviors.description: Past the self-osc point the internal feedback paths saturate, warping the resonant sines into shaped/distorted waves. SPAN and BALANCE sculpt the resulting timbre.

behaviors.name: TITO self-modulation (SM)
behaviors.description: Each core audio-rate-modulates itself. Yields a radical, grainy, textured resonance character. Most audible with RESO in self-oscillation range.

behaviors.name: TITO cross-modulation (XM)
behaviors.description: The two cores modulate each other at audio rate, producing warbly/chirpy FM-like sidebands. BH and HH modes with XM and moderate SPAN give chaotic, unpredictable timbres.

behaviors.name: Notch prominence at low RESO
behaviors.trigger: MODE = NN / NL / HN · low RESO
behaviors.description: Counter-intuitively, notch modes sound deepest with RESO near zero — higher RESO narrows the notch until it is inaudible. Asymmetric BALANCE helps reveal the stopband in NL and HN.

behaviors.name: Vocal formant sweet spot
behaviors.trigger: MODE = LL / LB / BB · RESO 6–7 · SPAN 2–4
behaviors.description: The manual's recommended vocal-like setting. Two peaks land on typical formant ratios; sweeping FREQ scans vowel shapes. BALANCE adds consonant emphasis.

behaviors.name: Mode-dependent output level
behaviors.description: Different topologies have radically different gain. Narrow bandpass (HL) is loud, narrow notch (NN) is quiet. Expect to rebalance the mixer after switching modes.

behaviors.name: Input overdrive via LEVEL
behaviors.description: The input stage progressively saturates before entering the cores. Useful for adding grit without relying on RESO or TITO.

patchIdeas.name: Vocal formant sweep
patchIdeas.patch: BB mode with two peaks tuned to formant ratios; a slow LFO scans FREQ for a vowel sweep, BALANCE CV opens consonant emphasis.
steps: Patch your VCO (saw or pulse — harmonic-rich) → Belgrad INPUT.
Set Belgrad MODE rotary to BB (the manual's vocal mode).
Set LEVEL to ~6 (clean), RESO to 7 (just below self-osc), SPAN knob to 3.
Set BALANCE knob to noon (symmetric peaks).
Push the FM slider up to about 4 (modest depth).
Patch a slow LFO (~0.2 Hz triangle, ±5V) → Belgrad FM input.
Patch an envelope (~0.5 s decay) → Belgrad BALANCE input.
Trigger the envelope on each note — peaks tilt for consonant attack.
Patch Belgrad OUTPUT → Mixer In.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Belgrad OUTPUT — formant-filtered audio at modular level (≈±5V), level varies with MODE.
patchIdeas.hear: A vocal vowel sweep (oo → ah → ee) on each LFO cycle. The envelope on BALANCE adds a subtle consonant accent at note onset. Push SPAN higher for wider vowels.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Belgrad:
* MODE: BB
* RESO: 7
* SPAN: 3

- VCO (Out) -> Belgrad (INPUT)
- LFO (Out) >> Belgrad (FM) // vowel scan
- Envelope (Out) >> Belgrad (BALANCE) // consonant morph
- Belgrad (OUTPUT) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dual sine VCO from self-oscillation
patchIdeas.patch: No INPUT. RESO past self-osc gives two sine VCOs; SPAN sets the interval, BALANCE crossfades between them, V/OCT tracks pitch.
steps: Leave Belgrad INPUT unpatched (no audio source).
Set MODE to BB and TITO switch to center (OFF).
Set RESO to 8.5 — both cores self-oscillate as clean sines.
Set FREQ to noon, SPAN knob to 4 (≈ a few octaves between the two sines).
Set BALANCE to noon and the SPAN slider to 5.
Patch your sequencer's pitch CV → Belgrad V/OCT (tunes both sines, ~5 oct tracking).
Patch a slow LFO (~0.3 Hz, ±5V) → Belgrad BALANCE input.
Patch a static offset (~+3V) → Belgrad SPAN input to nudge the interval over time.
Patch Belgrad OUTPUT → VCA In, then to your mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Belgrad OUTPUT — two clean sine VCOs summed, modular level (≈±5V).
patchIdeas.hear: A dual-sine drone tracking the sequence. BALANCE LFO crossfades emphasis from the lower sine to the upper, like a slow tremolo between two pitches. Adjust SPAN to set the interval (unison to ~8 oct).
patchIdeas.diagram:
Belgrad:
* MODE: BB
* RESO: 8.5 (self-osc)
* TITO: OFF

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Belgrad (V/OCT)
- LFO (Out) >> Belgrad (BALANCE) // sine crossfade
- Static CV (Out) >> Belgrad (SPAN) // interval control
- Belgrad (OUTPUT) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: XM chaotic lead
patchIdeas.patch: HH mode with TITO=XM at self-osc and moderate SPAN — the manual's recipe for chaotic cross-modulated timbres.
steps: Leave Belgrad INPUT unpatched.
Set MODE to HH and flip the TITO switch DOWN (XM cross-modulation).
Set RESO to 8.5 (self-osc) and SPAN knob to 3 (moderate, per manual).
Set BALANCE to noon, LEVEL to 0.
Patch your keyboard CV → Belgrad V/OCT for pitch.
Patch an envelope (~0.4 s) → Belgrad SPAN input — opens timbre per note.
Patch a slow LFO → Belgrad BALANCE input for slow timbral drift.
Patch Belgrad OUTPUT → VCA In, gated by the same envelope.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Belgrad OUTPUT — chaotic cross-modulated lead, modular level (loud, watch your VCA gain).
patchIdeas.hear: A warbly, chirpy lead full of FM-like sidebands. Each note opens with an evolving inharmonic burst as the envelope sweeps SPAN; BALANCE drift keeps the timbre moving. Tame SPAN if it gets too unpredictable.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Belgrad:
* MODE: HH
* TITO: XM
* RESO: 8.5
* SPAN: ~3 (moderate)

- Keyboard (CV) p> Belgrad (V/OCT)
- Envelope (Out) >> Belgrad (SPAN) // timbre opens
- LFO (Out) >> Belgrad (BALANCE)
- Belgrad (OUTPUT) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Pingable highpass kick
patchIdeas.patch: HH mode just below self-osc — triggers into FM ping the highpass peak, V/OCT sets the pitch of the zap.
steps: Leave Belgrad INPUT unpatched.
Set MODE to HH, TITO to center (OFF).
Set RESO to ~7.8 (just below self-osc — needs to ring, not sustain).
Set FREQ to noon, SPAN knob to 0 (single peak for a tight ping).
Push the FM slider up to ~7 (deep enough to excite the peak).
Patch your clock or trigger source → Belgrad FM input — each pulse pings the peak.
Patch a pitch CV (or static offset) → Belgrad V/OCT to set the ping pitch.
Patch Belgrad OUTPUT → VCA In.
Patch a short envelope (~0.2 s decay) → VCA CV to shape the kick body.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Belgrad OUTPUT — short percussive pinged sine at the FREQ pitch, modular level.
patchIdeas.hear: A zappy, electronic-kick-like thump with a high-end snap. Pitch tracks V/OCT; lower FREQ for a rounder kick, raise it for a chirpy zap. Nudge RESO closer to 8 for a longer ringing tail.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Belgrad:
* MODE: HH
* TITO: OFF
* RESO: ~7.8

- Clock (Out) t> Belgrad (FM) // ping
- Pitch CV (Out) p> Belgrad (V/OCT)
- Belgrad (OUTPUT) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Short phaser (double notch)
patchIdeas.patch: NN mode with RESO low (deepest notches per manual); a slow LFO on SPAN moves the two notches like a phaser.
steps: Patch a pad VCO or any harmonic-rich source → Belgrad INPUT.
Set MODE to NN (double notch).
Set RESO knob to 1 — manual: notches are deepest at LOW reso.
Set FREQ to noon, SPAN knob to 4, BALANCE to noon.
Push the SPAN slider up to ~6 (depth of LFO motion).
Patch a slow LFO (~0.2 Hz triangle, ±5V) → Belgrad SPAN input — notch spacing breathes.
Patch a small offset (~+1V) → Belgrad BALANCE input for asymmetric sweep (one notch deeper).
Patch Belgrad OUTPUT → Reverb In, then to mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Belgrad OUTPUT — the dry pad with two moving notches carved out, modular level.
patchIdeas.hear: A short phaser-like swoosh as the two notches glide apart and back together. BALANCE asymmetry makes one notch dominant for an off-center sweep. Stays subtle — push RESO past ~3 and the notches tighten and disappear.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Belgrad:
* MODE: NN
* RESO: 1

- Pad VCO (Out) -> Belgrad (INPUT)
- Slow LFO (Out) >> Belgrad (SPAN) // notch spacing
- Offset (Out) >> Belgrad (BALANCE) // asymmetry
- Belgrad (OUTPUT) -> Reverb (In)

patchIdeas.name: SM resonance grain on drums
patchIdeas.patch: LB mode with TITO=SM and overdriven LEVEL — drums get a textured, growling resonant flavour with stepped formants from S&H.
steps: Patch your drum bus → Belgrad INPUT.
Set MODE to LB, flip TITO switch UP (SM self-modulation).
Set LEVEL to 7 (into overdrive per manual), RESO to 8.
Set FREQ to noon, SPAN knob to 4, BALANCE to noon.
Push the FM slider to ~5 and SPAN slider to ~6.
Patch an envelope follower from the drum bus → Belgrad FM input — louder hits modulate cutoff.
Patch a clocked S&H (clock from the drum sequencer, noise into S&H input) → Belgrad SPAN input.
Patch Belgrad OUTPUT → Mixer (parallel-blend with the dry drums for control).
patchIdeas.signalOut: Belgrad OUTPUT — overdriven, resonance-textured drum bus at modular level (loud — attenuate at the mixer).
patchIdeas.hear: Drums gain a gritty, vocal-like growl. Each hit ducks and warps the resonance via SM; the S&H steps the formant peaks per beat for a stuttering, rhythmic timbral shift. Blend with the dry bus to keep transients.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Belgrad:
* MODE: LB
* TITO: SM
* RESO: 8
* LEVEL: 7

- Drum Bus (Out) -> Belgrad (INPUT)
- Env Follower (Out) >> Belgrad (FM)
- S&H (Out) >> Belgrad (SPAN) // stepped formants
- Belgrad (OUTPUT) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo-like dual-peak spread
patchIdeas.patch: LH mode with wide SPAN — two opposing LFOs pan the lower and upper peaks across an external stereo mixer for pseudo-stereo from a mono source.
steps: Patch a pad VCO → Belgrad INPUT.
Set MODE to LH (lowpass + highpass band-reject).
Set RESO to 6, SPAN knob to 8 (wide stopband, peaks far apart).
Set BALANCE to noon, LEVEL to ~6.
Patch a slow LFO A (~0.1 Hz triangle, ±5V) → Belgrad BALANCE input — tilts emphasis L↔R.
Patch the inverted output of LFO A (LFO B) → your stereo mixer's pan CV input.
Patch Belgrad OUTPUT → Mixer In (the channel whose pan is being modulated).
Pan modulation + BALANCE tilt creates the illusion of two peaks moving across the stereo field.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Belgrad OUTPUT (mono) → modulated mixer pan = pseudo-stereo image at line/modular level.
patchIdeas.hear: A wide, drifting filtered pad that feels stereo despite the mono source. The lower peak appears to swing left while the upper swings right (and vice versa) as BALANCE tilts and the mixer pan sweeps in opposition.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Belgrad:
* MODE: LH
* RESO: 6
* SPAN: 8

- Pad VCO (Out) -> Belgrad (INPUT)
- LFO A (Out) >> Belgrad (BALANCE) // peak tilt L
- LFO B (inv) >> Mixer.Pan (CV) // peak tilt R
- Belgrad (OUTPUT) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Formant arp via BALANCE CV
patchIdeas.patch: BB mode held at one root pitch; clocked S&H steps BALANCE for a two-formant arpeggio from a static drone.
steps: Patch a drone VCO (saw or square) → Belgrad INPUT.
Set MODE to BB, RESO to 7, SPAN knob to 3 (vocal sweet spot).
Set FREQ to a musical root note, BALANCE to noon, LEVEL to ~6.
Patch a static root-pitch CV → Belgrad V/OCT (holds the root steady).
Patch your clock → S&H trigger input.
Patch noise → S&H signal input.
Patch S&H output → Belgrad BALANCE input — each clock step jumps to a new peak emphasis.
Patch Belgrad OUTPUT → Mixer In.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Belgrad OUTPUT — drone with stepped two-formant emphasis, modular level.
patchIdeas.hear: A static drone that arpeggiates between vowel-like timbres. Each clock pulse the random S&H value picks a new BALANCE position, jumping between lower-peak and upper-peak emphasis — like an arpeggiator but on formant emphasis instead of pitch.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Belgrad:
* MODE: BB
* RESO: 7
* SPAN: 3

- Drone VCO (Out) -> Belgrad (INPUT)
- Root CV (Out) p> Belgrad (V/OCT)
- Clock (Out) c> S&H (Trig)
- Noise (Out) >> S&H (In)
- S&H (Out) >> Belgrad (BALANCE) // formant step
- Belgrad (OUTPUT) -> Mixer (In)
